LAW AND MOTION, 302, PLAINTIFF SARAH PERLISS'S MOTION TO ADD MONICA MAZZEI AS A CO-DEBTOR TO THE JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE RECORD DISCLOSES THAT MR. POTTER AND MS. MAZZEI DID NOT HAVE ANY COMMUNITY PROPERTY AND THUS THERE IS NO BASIS TO ADD MS. MAZZEI AS A JUDGMENT DEBTOR. MS. MAZZEI IS CORRECT THAT LITKE O'FARRELL, LLC V. TIPTON (2012) 204 CAL.APP.4TH 1178 REQUIRES THE DENIAL OF THIS MOTION. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 =302/HK

Motion To Add Monica Mazzei (Formerly Monica Mazzei Potter) As A Co Debtor And Notice Of Hearing On Motion
Off Calendar Jan-13-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Plaintiff Sarah Perliss's motion to add Monica Mazzei as a co-debtor to the judgment is denied. The record discloses that Mr. Potter and Ms. Mazzei did not have any community property and thus there is no basis to add Ms. Mazzei as a judgment debtor. Ms. Mazzei is correct that Litke O'Farrell, LLC v. Tipton (2012) 204 Cal.App.4th 1178 requires the denial of this motion. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/hk

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement And Award Attorney Fees
Off Calendar Sep-23-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Andrew S. Cantor, Esq. (415-817-1708) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

Attorney, Brett L. Gibbs of Weisberg & Miller Law Firm (415-296-7070) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification.

PLAINTIFF SARAH PERLISS' Motion To Enforce Settlement And Award Attorney Fees is GRANTED. Defendants breached the agreement by failing to pay $85,000 called for in the agreement. Plaintiff did not breach the confidentiality portion of the agreement and any breach of the statement portion of the agreement did not excuse defendants' obligation to make the payment. Plaintiff is awarded fees and costs of $6000 for a total due to plaintiff from defendants of $91,000. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR #5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

Notice Of Motion To Strike, And Motion To Strike, Portions Of Plaintiff Sarah Perliss Amended Complaint By Defendants Scott Potter And Skp Capital Partners
Off Calendar Feb-11-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANTS SCOTT POTTER AND SKP CAPITAL PARTNERS' Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff Sarah Perliss Amended Complaint By Defendants Scott Potter And SKP Capital Partners, Defendants' motion to strike the punitive damages allegations is granted with twenty days leave to amend. The first amended complaint does not adequately allege entitlement to punitive damages due to the inadequacy of the allegations supporting the intentional fraud claims. As stated in the ruling on defendants' demurrer, plaintiff is given leave to allege her fraud claims with the required specificity. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-11-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANTS SCOTT POTTER AND SKP CAPITAL PARTNERS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT, Defendants' demurrer is sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the first, third, fifth and sixth cause of action in the first amended complaint and sustained without leave to amend as to the fourth, eighth and ninth causes of action in the first amended complaint. The fraud causes of action (first, fifth and sixth) are not pled with the required specificity and plaintiff is given an opportunity to do so. The negligence cause of action (third) does not allege breach of any duty of care separate from the alleged contract, but it is not clear whether plaintiff may be able to do. There is no cognizable basis for either a breach of fiduciary duty or conversion claim on these facts and thus those claims (fourth and eighth) are sustained without leave to amend. Imposition of constructive trust is a remedy, not a claim, and thus the purported claim labeled as such (ninth) is sustained without leave to amend. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
